title: Copy, Replace, Rename, Delete, Batch, and Concurrency · Runtime Recall
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs copy, replace, rename, delete, batch, and concurrency from browser, Go service, S3, CloudFront, security, billing, and stakeholder evidence, then challenges one retained misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for conditional object-mutation protocol after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Copy or replace with explicit source version, conditional evidence, metadata directive, encryption, checksum, tags, and destination validation.
Implement rename as version-aware copy, validation, metadata update, reference switch, and safe source deletion with recoverable intermediate states.
Use validators, version IDs, idempotency records, or application locks to detect stale concurrent mutation and expose 409 or 412 meaning.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Copy Replace Metadata · changed case · Review Copy Replace Metadata
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Go file-delivery team handles case 5953 during a compliance restore drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to model a non-atomic version-aware copy, validate, and delete rename protocol; keep browser execution deterministic and move AWS claims to an authorized transfer gate; then change region, range, or signed header and explain the exact failure boundary. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Which action demonstrates Copy Replace Metadata without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Copy Replace Metadata · changed case · Review Copy Replace Metadata.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for copy or replace with explicit source version, conditional evidence, metadata directive, encryption, checksum, tags, and destination validation. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Copy Replace Metadata · changed case · Review Copy Replace Metadata, keep Copy Replace Metadata connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Chunk batch operations, correlate per-object outcomes, retry only eligible items, preserve authorization, and report partial success accessibly.

hints: For S3, CloudFront, and Go Record what required effort · Review Batch Partial Failure, name the stakeholder, file, object version, authorization, byte, time, concurrency, cache, cost, cleanup, and recovery boundary, Batch Partial Failure precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At S3, CloudFront, and Go Record what required effort · Review Batch Partial Failure, trace this concrete input and predict runtime validation, authorization, custody, integrity, range, stream, cache, retry, cost, cleanup, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A review Go file-delivery team handles case 8775 during an SDK and infrastructure canary. Build deterministic Go evidence to model a non-atomic version-aware copy, validate, and delete rename protocol; keep browser execution deterministic and move AWS claims to an authorized transfer gate; then cancel halfway through and prove every body, part, and goroutine reaches a terminal state. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Chunk batch operations, correlate per-object outcomes, retry only eligible items, preserve authorization, and report partial success accessibly.

For S3, CloudFront, and Go Record what required effort · Review Batch Partial Failure, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, file, object, policy, transfer, integrity, cache, fault, cost, restore, and controlled AWS transfer evidence: One successful batch HTTP response means every requested object changed.
